verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 encodes TGN46 (also known as TGN38 in rodents), a type I single-pass transmembrane protein that cycles between the trans-Golgi network and the plasma membrane. Although TGN46 transiently visits the cell surface before rapid endocytic retrieval back to the TGN, the steady-state distribution is overwhelmingly in the TGN/Golgi compartment (>95% intracellular at steady state). Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — surface display is not selectively induced by stress/activation; it reflects constitutive rapid cycling. (2) tissue_restricted_surface — not restricted to a narrow lineage. (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no evidence for lysosomal exocytosis route. (4) dual_localization — the brief PM transit is extremely transient (half-life at surface ~10 min) and not a stable PM pool; this is a retrieval cycle, not dual localization. (5) stable_surface_attachment — it is not a secreted/shed protein anchored to a partner. Given the dominant endomembrane residency and the highly transient nature of PM appearance, this best fits endomembrane_resident rather than dual_localization.
predicted_key_uncertainty: TGN46 does transiently appear at the PM during recycling; whether the brief surface exposure window is sufficient for therapeutic targeting (e.g., rapid-internalization ADC) is debatable, potentially warranting contextual/dual_localization classification.
